# ObservabilityOS — AI-Native DevOps Intelligence & Log Analytics

ObservabilityOS is an open-source, developer-first observability and log analytics platform that automatically scrubs PII data locally, analyzes error rates and response latency using standard deviation Z-Scores in real-time, and compiles AI incident post-mortems using LLMs (GPT-4 and Claude).

## Core System Architecture

- **Ingestion Protocol**: Compliant with OpenTelemetry (OTLP) HTTP/JSON standards.
- **Local Scrubbing**: PII scrubbing is executed on client machines using standard patterns (redacts passwords, tokens, auth headers, credit cards, custom regex) before telemetry is transmitted.
- **Storage Strategy**: Structured telemetry log lines are written to a MongoDB database (backed by indexing for high-speed retrieval), and Redis serves as a rate-limiting and metadata caching layer.
- **Incident Correlator**: Subscribes to GitHub release webhooks to match deployment events (author, commit SHA, files changed) to the exact timestamp of Z-score anomaly spikes.

## Integration & Getting Started

Refer to the main documentation hosted at https://docs.observabilityos.in.

### JavaScript/TypeScript SDK Integration
```typescript
import { logger } from "@observability-os/sdk";

// Automatically begins local batching ring-buffer operations
logger.info("Service initialized", { environment: "production" });
```

### Docker sidecar Configuration
For non-JS environments, mount local logs or redirect container standard out (stdout) streams into the sidecar daemon.
